progress.searching: Searching for MA Touch thermostats… This continues automatically and advances as soon as one appears. Make sure your thermostats are powered and within range of a Bluetooth proxy.

pick.title: Add Mitsubishi MA Touch thermostats
pick.description: Select the thermostats to add. They are discovered automatically by their CT01MA name via your Bluetooth proxies.

retry.title: No thermostats found yet
retry.description: No MA Touch thermostats are visible yet. Make sure they are powered and within range of a Bluetooth proxy, then submit to search again.

device.title: Configure thermostat
device.description: Set a name and the PIN for {device}. Find the PIN on the thermostat under Settings → Bluetooth (enable Bluetooth in the admin settings first; default code 0000).

abort.no_devices_found: No MA Touch thermostats found nearby. Make sure they are powered and within range of a Bluetooth proxy, then try again.
abort.already_configured: Mitsubishi MA Touch is already set up. To add another thermostat, open the existing entry and use the 'Add thermostat' button — you don't need a second entry.

user.title: Add thermostat
user.description: Select a thermostat and set its name and PIN. If none are listed yet, submit to search again.

error.no_devices_found: No new MA Touch thermostats are visible yet. Make sure they are powered and within range of a Bluetooth proxy, then submit to search again.

data.log_polls: Log every poll to the telemetry file (verbose)

invalid_pin.title: {name}: incorrect PIN
invalid_pin.description:
Home Assistant couldn't authenticate to **{name}** — its PIN was rejected, so the thermostat can't be controlled.

To fix it, open **Settings → Devices & Services → Mitsubishi MA Touch**, find **{name}**, and choose **Reconfigure** to enter the correct PIN. The PIN is on the thermostat under **Settings → Bluetooth** (enable Bluetooth in the admin settings first; default code 0000).

This alert clears automatically once the correct PIN connects.

wedged.title: {name}: reachable but won't connect
wedged.description:
Home Assistant can see **{name}** advertising over Bluetooth (a proxy is in range), but every attempt to connect to it has failed for several minutes — so it can't be read or controlled. Your other thermostats are connecting normally.

This almost always means the thermostat's Bluetooth radio has wedged. **Power-cycle the thermostat** — cut power to it for about 30 seconds (e.g. flip the breaker for that air handler, or disconnect and reseat the controller) — to reset its radio. It should reconnect within a minute.

If power-cycling doesn't help, make sure a Bluetooth proxy is close enough for a solid signal. This alert clears automatically once the thermostat reconnects.
